prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for nitrobenzene (14 atoms: 2 O, 1 N, 6 C, 5 H) computed at DFT tier 'standard' — B3LYP/def2-TZVP (PySCF RKS, density-fit RI) — gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: user-supplied nitrobenzene.xyz, taken as-is (no re-optimization). N±1 states: cation (charge +1, mult 2), anion (charge −1, mult 2). Partial charges via Mulliken (PySCF). SCF converged; integrity status ok; trustworthy: true; sum(f+) = 1.0000; sum(f−) = 1.0000. No warnings reported.

Headline result: the most electrophilic atom is Atom 4 (C, ipso carbon bearing –NO₂) with f⁺ = 0.49641781. The most nucleophilic atom is Atom 5 (C, ortho carbon) with f⁻ = 0.10729. The –NO₂ oxygens (atoms 1 & 2) each show f⁺ ≈ 0.473, consistent with their electron-withdrawing character. The dual descriptor for the ipso carbon is +0.51071, the largest in the molecule, classifying it as the strongest electrophilic center. No warnings were reported — this was a clean, fully converged run.
